Tests that multiplying `Number.MAX_VALUE` by 2 results in `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript's `Number` object when multiplying `Number.MAX_VALUE` by 2. The test verifies that the result of this operation is `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY`, which confirms that JavaScript correctly handles numeric overflow by returning positive infinity when a number exceeds the maximum representable value. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing specifically on validating the behavior of numeric overflow in JavaScript.
